bull.b: Protalix’s proprietary plant cell–based ProCellEx platform and in-house manufacturing that already supports marketed products create a durable competitive advantage. Owning production enables supply control, lower incremental costs for follow-on biologics, and makes the company an attractive partner for licensing and p

A $25M milestone boosted cash to $51M and the company reports no debt or warrants, materially improving financial flexibility. This liquidity supports continued clinical programs, reimbursement rollouts, and commercialization investments without immediate financing, reducing near-term dilution and execution risk.

Recent TTM performance shows a meaningful step-up: strong revenue growth and materially expanded gross and net margins. If sustainable, higher margins reflect operational leverage from product sales and lower cost of goods, improving free cash flow generation capacity and enabling reinvestment into R&D and commercializ

bear.b: Historical volatility in operating and free cash flow highlights dependence on episodic events (e.g., milestones) and timing of partner purchases. This lumpiness reduces visibility on the company’s ability to consistently fund programs and operations without recurring external payments or capital raises.

A meaningful portion of revenue and near-term profitability is tied to partner payments (milestones, profit shares, royalties) and a small set of products. That concentration creates structural risk: partner demand, contract terms, or milestone timing can swing revenue materially and compress predictability over the me

Product sales decline and reliance on partner-led reimbursement rollouts create execution risk for commercial uptake. Separately, PRX-115 enrollment and timing remain uncertain. Execution delays or weaker-than-expected adoption would pressure revenue sustainability and extend the timeline to realize pipeline value.
